title: 2.4 Reproduction in Plants
description: Reproduction in plants — every fact from the textbook.

questions.question: What group of non-flowering plants are gymnosperms classified as?
questions.correctAnswer: Higher seed-bearing non-flowering plants

questions.question: What type of trees are common gymnosperms in Ethiopia mentioned in the textbook?
questions.correctAnswer: Podocarpus falcatus (Zigba) and Juniperus procera (Tid)

questions.question: What group of gymnosperms do pine trees belong to?
questions.correctAnswer: Conifers

questions.question: Does a pine tree have male and female cones on one plant or separate plants?
options: Both male and female cones on one plant
questions.correctAnswer: Both male and female cones on one plant

questions.question: What is the process called when pollen is transferred from the male cone to the female cone in pine trees?
questions.correctAnswer: Pollination

questions.question: What helps pollination in pine trees?
questions.correctAnswer: Wind

questions.question: What forms after pollination in pine trees?
questions.correctAnswer: Pollen tube

questions.question: What does the generative cell of the pollen grain divide into as it approaches the female cone?
questions.correctAnswer: Two sperm cells

questions.question: How many ovules does the female cone contain per scale?
questions.correctAnswer: Two

questions.question: How many surviving cells remain after meiosis in each ovule?
questions.correctAnswer: One

questions.question: What is an archegonium?
options: A reproductive structure that contains a single large egg
questions.correctAnswer: A reproductive structure that contains a single large egg

questions.question: What is formed when one sperm cell fuses with the egg in gymnosperms?
questions.correctAnswer: A zygote

questions.question: What does a zygote in gymnosperms develop into?
options: A seed embryo inside the female cone
questions.correctAnswer: A seed embryo inside the female cone

questions.question: How is a mature seed liberated from the female cone?
options: Upon drying and opening of the female cone
questions.correctAnswer: Upon drying and opening of the female cone

questions.question: What shape are the leaves of most conifers?
options: Narrow and needle-shaped with thick cuticle
questions.correctAnswer: Narrow and needle-shaped with thick cuticle

questions.question: What enables conifers to live in extremely cold and hot environments?
options: Their narrow needle-shaped leaves with thick cuticle
questions.correctAnswer: Their narrow needle-shaped leaves with thick cuticle

questions.question: Are conifers evergreen or deciduous?
questions.correctAnswer: Evergreen

questions.question: Do conifers shade their leaves in winter and summer?
options: No, they do not shade their leaves in winter or summer
questions.correctAnswer: No, they do not shade their leaves in winter or summer

questions.question: What is the flower in angiosperms?
questions.correctAnswer: The reproductive organ

questions.question: What are the four floral parts of a typical flower?
questions.correctAnswer: Sepals, petals, stamen and pistil

questions.question: What is the function of sepals?
options: Green leaf-like structure protecting the lower part of female and male parts
questions.correctAnswer: Green leaf-like structure protecting the lower part of female and male parts

questions.question: What is the collective term for all sepals?
questions.correctAnswer: Calyx

questions.question: What is the function of petals?
options: Mostly brightly coloured to attract pollinating agents like insects
questions.correctAnswer: Mostly brightly coloured to attract pollinating agents like insects

questions.question: What is the collective term for all petals?
questions.correctAnswer: Corolla

questions.question: What is the male part of a flower called?
questions.correctAnswer: Stamen (Androecium)

questions.question: What are the parts of the stamen?
questions.correctAnswer: Filament and bilobed anther

questions.question: What is the female part of a flower called?
questions.correctAnswer: Pistil (Gynoecium or carpel)

questions.question: What are the parts of the pistil?
options: Ovary with ovules, style and stigma
questions.correctAnswer: Ovary with ovules, style and stigma

questions.question: What is a complete flower?
options: A flower that has only male parts
A flower that has all four floral parts
questions.correctAnswer: A flower that has all four floral parts

questions.question: What is an incomplete flower?
options: A flower that has all floral parts
A flower that does not have any one of the four floral parts
A flower that has only female parts
A flower that has only male parts
questions.correctAnswer: A flower that does not have any one of the four floral parts

questions.question: What is a perfect flower?
options: A flower that has only sepals and petals
A flower that has both stamen and pistil
questions.correctAnswer: A flower that has both stamen and pistil

questions.question: What is an imperfect flower?
options: A flower that has both male and female parts
A flower that does not have either stamen or pistil
A flower that has all four floral parts
A flower that is brightly coloured
questions.correctAnswer: A flower that does not have either stamen or pistil

questions.question: What is a pistillate flower?
options: A flower that has stamen but no pistil
A flower that has pistil but no stamen
A flower that has both stamen and pistil
A flower that has no floral parts
questions.correctAnswer: A flower that has pistil but no stamen

questions.question: What is a staminate flower?
options: A flower that has pistil but no stamen
A flower that has stamen but no pistil
A flower that has all floral parts
questions.correctAnswer: A flower that has stamen but no pistil

questions.question: What is pollination?
options: The transfer of pollen grains from the anther of a stamen to the stigma of the pistil
questions.correctAnswer: The transfer of pollen grains from the anther of a stamen to the stigma of the pistil

questions.question: What is self-pollination?
options: Transfer between flowers on different plants
Transfer between stamen and pistil on one flower or between flowers on one plant
questions.correctAnswer: Transfer between stamen and pistil on one flower or between flowers on one plant

questions.question: What is cross-pollination?
options: Transfer between two flowers on different plants
questions.correctAnswer: Transfer between two flowers on different plants

questions.question: What do pollen grains landing on the stigma form?
options: Pollen tubes that grow down the style
questions.correctAnswer: Pollen tubes that grow down the style

questions.question: Where does fertilization occur in flowering plants?
options: In the ovule within the ovary
questions.correctAnswer: In the ovule within the ovary

questions.question: What is formed as a result of fertilization?
options: A zygote that develops into a seed embryo
questions.correctAnswer: A zygote that develops into a seed embryo

questions.question: What is unique about fertilization in angiosperms?
options: They exhibit double fertilization, forming endosperm
questions.correctAnswer: They exhibit double fertilization, forming endosperm

questions.question: What is the endosperm?
options: A reserve food storing structure formed by double fertilization
questions.correctAnswer: A reserve food storing structure formed by double fertilization

questions.question: What does the ovule mature into after fertilization?
questions.correctAnswer: A seed

questions.question: What does the ovary mature into after fertilization?
questions.correctAnswer: A fruit

questions.question: How are seeds described in relation to ovules and fruits in relation to ovaries?
options: Seed is a matured ovule while fruit is a matured ovary
Seed is a matured ovary while fruit is a matured ovule
Seed is a flower and fruit is a stem
questions.correctAnswer: Seed is a matured ovule while fruit is a matured ovary

questions.question: What is seed dispersal?
options: A mechanism of scattering seeds away from the parent plant
questions.correctAnswer: A mechanism of scattering seeds away from the parent plant

questions.question: What agents are involved in seed dispersal like pollination?
questions.correctAnswer: Animals or wind

questions.question: What is a dormant seed?
options: An inactive seed waiting for favourable conditions to start germination
A seed that has no embryo
questions.correctAnswer: An inactive seed waiting for favourable conditions to start germination

questions.question: What is seed germination?
options: The process where seed embryo starts to develop into a seedling
questions.correctAnswer: The process where seed embryo starts to develop into a seedling

questions.question: What breaks seed dormancy?
questions.correctAnswer: Enough water and nutrients
